mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing equity valuations to decline over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0%, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative returns, with directional accuracy below 45% over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during recovery phases with a t-statistic below 1.5, or the signal reverses direction in greater than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently near zero across 5-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive SPX returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal produces negative returns in greater than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.0 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and construction activity, dragging down broader economic growth and equity valuations over the quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1000.0 are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
